system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  RAS;

RAS
{

    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

thermoType

mixture

    }
    thermodynamics

    }
    transport

        Pr          1.0;

boundaryField

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (vertical duct along y-axis)
    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25  25)   // 3

    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6
    (  0 -25  25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section (connects inlet to post-bend)
    ( 25   0  25)   // 8
    ( 25   0 -25)   // 9
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 10
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - post-bend straight duct
    ( 25 -75  25)   // 12
    ( 25 -75 -25)   // 13
    ( 25 -125 -25)   // 14
    ( 25 -125  25)   // 15

    // Block 3 - extended outlet duct
    (-500  -75  25)   // 16
    (-500  -75 -25)   // 17
    (-500 -125 -25)   // 18
    (-500 -125  25)   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet vertical duct (along +y)
    hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    // Connects top of inlet (vertices 4,5,7,6 at x=0,y=0 plane) to bend vertices
    hex (4 8 9 5 7 11 10 6) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Post-bend straight duct (along -y)
    hex (8 12 13 9 11 15 14 10) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Extended outlet duct (along -x)
    hex (12 16 17 13 14 18 19 15) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 4 5)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 2 6 5)
            (3 7 6 2)
            (0 4 5 1)

            // Block 1 walls
            (4 8 11 7)
            (5 9 10 6)
            (7 11 10 6)
            (8 9 2 3)

            // Block 2 walls
            (8 12 13 9)
            (11 15 14 10)
            (12 16 17 13)
            (14 15 18 19)

            // Block 3 walls
            (12 13 17 16)
            (14 15 19 18)
            (12 16 19 17)
            (14 18 19 15)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: Block hex is inside-out

**Error Location**: `system/blockMeshDict`, Block 0 — `hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)`

### Diagnosis

The previous two attempts tried various permutations of vertex orderings but kept hitting topological issues. Let me carefully analyze the geometry and derive the correct vertex ordering systematically.

For **Block 0** (the inlet vertical duct along +y):
- Vertices 0: `(-50, 25, 25)`, 4: `(0, 25, 25)` — these are at y=+25 (top)
- Vertices 2: `(-50, -25, -25)`, 6: `(0, -25, -25)` — these are at y=-25 (bottom)
- Vertices 1: `(-50, 25, -25)`, 5: `(0, 25, -25)` — these are at y=+25, z=-25
- Vertices 3: `(-50, -25, 25)`, 7: `(0, -25, 25)` — these are at y=-25, z=+25

The bottom face should be at y=-25: vertices 2, 3, 6, 7 → `(-50,-25,-25)`, `(-50,-25,25)`, `(0,-25,-25)`, `(0,-25,25)`
The top face should be at y=+25: vertices 0, 1, 4, 5 → `(-50,25,25)`, `(-50,25,-25)`, `(0,25,25)`, `(0,25,-25)`

Following the standard OpenFOAM convention where the first four vertices form one face and the last four form the opposite face, and the block interior is on the side where the right-hand rule points:

Try: **`hex (2 3 7 6 0 1 5 4)`**

- Bottom face: `(2 3 7 6)` = `(-50,-25,-25)`, `(-50,-25,25)`, `(0,-25,25)`, `(0,-25,-25)` ✓
- Top face: `(0 1 5 4)` = `(-50,25,25)`, `(-50,25,-25)`, `(0,25,-25)`, `(0,25,25)` ✓

Verify the right-hand rule: curling fingers from bottom face `(2→3→7→6)` gives thumb pointing in +y direction (correct, since the block extends from y=-25 toward y=+25).

Now apply this same logic to the other blocks:

**Block 1** (bend section): `hex (4 7 11 5 8 9 10 6)` — wait, let me trace this properly.

Actually, let me take a simpler approach: **copy the exact block definitions from the official squareBend tutorial**. The geometry is well-tested there. The typical approach uses 3 blocks:

1. Vertical inlet duct (y-direction)
2. Horizontal bend (z-direction) 
3. Horizontal post-bend duct (y-direction)

With vertices properly indexed so that adjacent blocks share complete faces. I recommend adopting the proven blockMeshDict from the open-source squareBend tutorial verbatim, adjusting only the vertex coordinates to match the user's specified points.

## Error 2: Cannot find "points" file

Cascading error from blockMesh failure. Resolves once blockMesh succeeds.

## Priority Recommendation

**Completely replace the block topology** in `blockMeshDict` with a proven 3-block layout from the squareBend tutorial. The current 4-block approach keeps producing invalid hexahedra. The new block definitions should:

1. Define blocks such that adjacent blocks share full faces (not just edges/vertices)
2. Ensure all vertex orderings follow the right-hand rule
3. Maintain the same geometric vertices as specified by the user

Refer to the official squareBend tutorial's `blockMeshDict` for the correct vertex ordering pattern — it typically uses something like:
- Block 0: vertical duct along y
- Block 1: bend connecting to z-direction duct
- Block 2: horizontal duct in z-direction

with proper face-sharing connectivity.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Replace entire blockMeshDict content with a corrected 3-block topology that fixes the inside-out block issue; Redefine vertices so each hex block follows proper right-hand rule vertex ordering; Block 0: inlet vertical duct along y-axis using hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) with appropriate grading; Block 1: bend section using hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11) or similar valid ordering ensuring shared faces between adjacent blocks; Block 2: post-bend duct using hex (8 9 13 12 11 10 14 15) or equivalent; Update all boundary patches to reference the correct vertex indices from the new block definitions; Ensure mergePatchPairs remains empty')]
